title: Ligusticopsis tenuisecta
taxonomy: Ligusticopsis tenuisecta belongs to the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It is a member of the genus Ligusticopsis, a group of flowering plants native to the Himalayan region. This species is further classified as an annual or biennial herb, which is a characteristic of its growth habit. The classification of Ligusticopsis tenuisecta is also supported by its morphological features, such as its leaves and flowers. Further research is needed to determine the exact relationship of Ligusticopsis tenuisecta to other species within the family Apiaceae.

The plant is said to have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. In traditional Chinese medicine, Ligusticopsis tenuisecta is believed to have a balancing effect on the body's "qi" and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis and respiratory problems.
